cards.front: What does a logic inverter do?
cards.back: Outputs the logical complement: HIGH → LOW, LOW → HIGH. Vout = NOT(Vin). The simplest logic gate.

cards.front: What are the two parts of every MOS inverter?
cards.back: 1. Driver transistor (NMOS enhancement, controlled by input). 2. Load element (can be resistor, enhancement MOSFET, or depletion MOSFET).
VDD − VT (degraded). The enhancement load turns off when VGS drops below VT, so output can never reach full VDD. This is the main weakness.
Full VDD. The depletion load has VGS = 0 (gate tied to source), so it stays ON as a depletion device even as Vout → VDD. No threshold voltage loss.

cards.front: Why is a MOSFET load better than a resistor in ICs?
cards.back: MOSFET takes much less chip area than a resistor of equivalent value, uses the same fabrication process, and provides better performance (especially depletion load as current source).
Power wasted when output is LOW: P = VDD × IDS. Both load and driver are ON, creating a DC current path from VDD to ground. This is the main problem with NMOS/PMOS logic.

cards.front: What are the logic levels in MOS circuits?
cards.back: V(0) ≈ 0 V (ground). V(1) ≈ VDD (positive supply). A good logic family has outputs as close to these ideals as possible.

cards.front: When Vin = HIGH in a MOS inverter, what happens?
cards.back: Driver (T1) turns ON with low Ron. Current flows VDD → Load → Driver → GND. Voltage divider action pulls Vout close to 0 V (LOW).

cards.front: When Vin = LOW in a MOS inverter, what happens?
cards.back: Driver (T1) turns OFF (no current path). Load pulls output toward VDD. Enhancement load: reaches VDD−VT. Depletion load: reaches full VDD.

cards.front: How does the depletion load achieve constant-current behaviour?
cards.back: Gate tied to source → VGS = 0. Depletion device is ON at VGS = 0, conducting IDSS. Current is independent of Vout (within saturation), acting as a current source.

cards.front: What logic family solves the static power problem?
cards.back: CMOS (Complementary MOS). By pairing NMOS and PMOS, one transistor is always OFF in every logic state, breaking the VDD-to-ground current path.

cards.front: Compare the three MOS inverter load types.
cards.back: Resistor: good swing but huge area. Enhancement: small area but degraded HIGH (VDD−VT). Depletion: small area + full VDD swing + current-source behaviour. Best NMOS option.
